description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, flared metallic horn in a pale, slightly tarnished finish, mounted on a dark wooden base. The base is ornately shaped with curved edges and features a decorative inlay on the side, as well as a small digital display and two control knobs on the front. The turntable platter is black and circular, holding a worn, light-colored vinyl record with a visible label at its center. The tonearm is metallic and curves elegantly from the side, positioned over the record, with a visible stylus at its end. The overall design combines antique aesthetics with subtle modern touches, such as the digital display. The object appears robust and is likely constructed from wood, metal, and plastic components.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large metallic horn, ornate wooden base, and a black turntable platter holding a vinyl record. Features include a digital display, control knobs, and a curved metallic tonearm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large flared horn, digital display, and two control knobs on the wooden base, with the turntable platter and record clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the decorative inlay on the wooden base and the full curvature of the metallic horn, with the record and platter partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the back of the horn, the tonearm's attachment, and the back side of the base, with less detail on the control panel.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the circular shape of the horn opening, the full platter, and the record's label, with minimal visibility of the base's front features.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying a central position on the wooden base. It is slightly recessed and holds a vinyl record with a visible, worn label. The platter appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a matte finish and no visible grooves or patterns beyond the record itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the side of the base toward the center of the platter. It is medium in length, with a visible joint where it attaches to the base, and is positioned over the record. The finish is metallic and slightly tarnished, matching the horn.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible at the end of the tonearm, appearing as a small, pointed component. It is attached to the tonearm and is likely made of metal or plastic, with a fine tip for contacting the record.
attribute_control_knobs: Two control knobs are visible on the front of the wooden base, circular in shape and metallic in appearance. They are positioned symmetrically on either side of a small digital display, with no visible labels or markings.
